posts.caption: Angi sells the same "lead" to a dozen guys, then you race each other to the bottom on price. We did the math on what a booked job actually costs you. It's worse than you think. 🔧
Pricing a job isn't a number you pull out of the air. It's a number you can defend when the homeowner flinches. Here's how to build one that holds.
Two shops, same town, same price. One's got 12 reviews, the other's got 140. You already know who gets the call. Here's how to close that gap — free.
The slow season isn't bad luck. It's a marketing problem you can see coming a mile away. Here's how to flatten it out before it hits.

We made a podcast. It's called Get Booked, Not F***ed — two old tradesmen and an idiot apprentice talking real shop marketing. No fluff, all bite, occasionally educational. Listen free. 🎧 @PlayMorePods #PodcastsOnAmazonMusic
Most contractors think 'marketing' is a logo and a boosted post. It's not. It's a machine: get found, get picked, get booked. Everything else is decoration. 🔧
Quoting, in one sentence. What would you add to the list? 👇
Your $35 lead isn't $35. Once you count the ones that never book, the real cost of a booked job is closer to $700. Here's the math nobody shows you.
Every shop has had this exact conversation. 🤝
Customers find you through 5 doors. If one lead app is most of your work, you don't own a business — you rent one. Spread out before they shut you off.
Cheap work isn't cheap. It's just paid for twice. Seen it?
There are 3 free spots at the top of every local search. Your Google Business Profile is the ticket in — and most contractors leave it half-empty.
What's the worst “the last guy did it” job you've walked into? Photos absolutely encouraged. We'll feature the best (worst) ones. 👇
98% of people leave a contractor website without calling. It's not the colors — it's that the phone number isn't a giant tap-to-call button. Fix that.
Found behind the drywall this week: a garden hose doing a P-trap's job. Rate the previous install 1–10. I'll start — it's on fire. 🔥
Before a customer ever hears your voice, they've judged you on your reviews. 140 recent reviews beats 12 — even at a higher price.
Drop the runner-up below. 👇
Should you pay for leads? Sometimes gold (LSA), sometimes a footrace (Angi). The test is the same: cost per BOOKED job, never the sticker price.
Plumbers, you're up first. The rest of us are not ready. 👇
Most contractors who 'tried Google Ads' just never answered the phone. Answer every call, start tiny, kill the losers. Here's how to not get burned.
It's somewhere in the truck. It's always somewhere in the truck.
A third of your future customers won't Google you — they'll ask ChatGPT who to hire. Good news: the same foundation wins both.
Every jobsite has “that guy.” What's his signature move? 👇
Five numbers, once a month, makes you impossible to fleece: cost per lead, cost per booked job, close rate, review count, response time.
Settle it in the comments. We're not picking sides. (Yes we are.) 👇
You don't lose most jobs on price. You lose them on response time. The first contractor to call back usually wins — even if he's worse than you.

Slow-season survival rule #1: the time to market is when you're busy, not when the phone stops. The work you book in summer is what fills January. What are you doing now to stay booked through winter? 👇
Your cheapest leads aren't on Angi. They're the customers you already made happy. Most contractors never ask — then complain leads are expensive.

Three ways shops bleed money they never see:

1. Unbilled change orders
2. “Quick favors” that aren't quick
3. Not charging for the drive

Which one's getting you this year? 👇
New episode of Get Booked, Not F***ed: why the guy with 140 reviews beats you even when he's worse — plus round 2 of Porno or Plumbing. 🎧 @PlayMorePods #PodcastsOnAmazonMusic

Posted for a helper. Three no-shows and one guy who quit because I asked him to sweep. The trades don't have a wage problem — they have a “show up” problem. Who's actually hiring right now? Drop your city. 👇
New episode: you don't lose jobs on price, you lose them on response time. 'Answer Your Phone' — speed to lead, plus a roofing company gets roasted. 🎧 @PlayMorePods #PodcastsOnAmazonMusic
Drop your best before/after. Any trade — roof, panel, re-pipe, install, cleanout. Let's see the work that doesn't get enough credit. 👇
